burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate in infants due to hospitalization risk
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in premature and high-risk infants
burden.source: WHO Europe/IHME lower respiratory infection context

vulnerablePopulations: infants with cardiac or pulmonary comorbidity
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of wheeze and respiratory distress; imaging only if severe or atypical

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care and oxygen when needed

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in pediatric care; hospital admission for hypoxia
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care, oxygen, hydration; RSV prophylaxis for high-risk infants where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Seasonal hospital crowding and RSV burden
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong pediatric care systems and standardized bronchiolitis pathways
